title: Specify lead reconciliation envelope
description: Validates the request and response envelopes of the agent-led lead-reconciliation step for lead reconciliation. `kind: request` is returned by `specify plan propose --dry-run --format json`: a flat catalog of raw, unmerged `(source, lead)` leads read 1:1 from `discovery.md`, plus the `projects[]` topology (always at least one project; each entry carries the normalized target adapter the kernel resolves on demand at read time — it is never written to `plan.yaml`). `kind: response` is the agent's grouping consumed by `specify plan propose --from`: a single `slices[]` list, each row carrying an explicit kebab-case `name`, its matched `sources[]` (at most one lead per source), and a bound `project` from the request (the kernel auto-binds the sole project when only one exists and resolves each slice's `target` on demand from the bound project's `projects[].target` rather than persisting it). The `scope` noun was removed (scope noun removed): there is no kernel fan-out grouping, so a body of work spanning more than one project is expressed as multiple slices joined by `depends-on`, and a lead may legally be referenced by more than one slice. The kernel validates this shape, enforces total lead coverage (every surveyed lead referenced by at least one slice), rejects same-source fusion per slice, enforces unique kebab-case slice names, and writes the structured `{ source, lead }` source bindings through the existing plan writers only when the plan is still replaceable; cross-source matching is agent judgment surfaced for Gate 1 review — the kernel never auto-merges leads. Closed top-level shape: unknown fields are rejected.

targetRef.description: Target adapter identifier, e.g. `omnia@v1` or `contracts@v1`. Mirrors `plan.schema.json` slice `target` pattern.

decision.description: One accepted Decision Record projected into routing identity. Title only — no body, Context, or Consequences prose is projected.

surfaceUnit.description: One baseline unit's projected surface: the unit slug and a bounded sample of its requirement titles.

requirements.description: Requirement-block headings of this unit's `spec.md`, in `REQ-NNN` id order, capped at K (default 8). Titles only.

more.description: Count of requirement titles elided past the cap. Absent when the unit fits within K.

platform.description: Closed target platform enum. `core` is the shared Rust business-logic crate; `ios`, `android` are supported shell platforms; `web`, `desktop` are future placeholders.

projectRef.description: One project the agent can bind a response slice to. For a workspace this is projected from the committed `.specify/topology.lock`; for a single regular project the CLI synthesizes one entry from `project.yaml` plus its own baseline. The agent binds a slice on the project's actual owned behaviour — its `target`, `description`, `surface[]`, and `recent[]` — not description prose or a hand-authored tag.

name.description: Registry project name; the value the kernel writes to `plan.yaml.slices[].project`.

target.description: The project's registered target adapter. The kernel resolves this value on demand for a slice bound to this project; it is not written to `plan.yaml` (a slice persists only its `project`).

description.description: Single-sentence domain characterisation used by the agent when more than one project shares a target.

surface.description: Deterministic baseline surface projected from the project's `.specify/specs/` through `.specify/topology.lock`: the units this project owns and a sample of each unit's requirement titles. The agent binds a slice on owned behaviour. Empty stays off the wire (greenfield routes on `description` alone).

recent.description: Recent per-merge outcome summaries from the project's journal ledger, newest activity last. Empty stays off the wire.

decisions.description: Accepted Decision Records projected from `.specify/decisions/` through `.specify/topology.lock`: the most recent K (default 8) in `DEC-NNNN` ascending order. The third routing-identity axis — why the project is shaped the way it is — so the agent can route a slice on architectural commitment and flag a lead that contradicts an accepted decision before Gate 1. Empty stays off the wire.

decisions-more.description: Count of accepted decisions elided past the K cap. Absent when the catalogue fits within K.

platforms.description: Target platforms this project builds for, projected from `project.yaml.platforms`. The agent reads each candidate project's platform set during reconciliation. Empty stays off the wire (non-platforms targets omit the field).

lead.description: Discovery lead id surfaced by this source binding. Identity is the `(source, lead)` pair; `lead` is not unique across `leads[]` when multiple sources surface the same slug.

synopsis.description: Reconciliation-grade per-source headline from this source's survey of the lead. Primary signal for agent cross-source grouping — SHOULD name the operation/surface and salient constraint; MAY span more than one line when one is too thin.

projects.description: Project topology the agent binds slices to — always at least one project, so there is no separate single-repo shape. For a workspace the entries are projected from the committed `.specify/topology.lock` (derived from each member project's `project.yaml`); for a single regular project the CLI synthesizes one entry from `project.yaml` (name + resolved target adapter + domain). Persisted project configuration is normalized into this envelope-local shape.

leads.description: Flat lead catalog: one row per raw `(source, lead)` lead, read 1:1 from `discovery.md` (no expansion or cross-source merge). The `lead` field repeats across rows when multiple sources surface the same slug.

source.description: Plan source binding key; must match a request catalog row.

lead.description: Discovery lead id; with `source`, must match a request catalog row.

name.description: Explicit plan slice name — required. The `scope` noun was removed (scope noun removed): with no kernel grouping the agent names every slice directly and the kernel writes the name verbatim to `plan.yaml.slices[].name`. A body of work that spans more than one project is expressed as multiple slices (which may legally reference the same lead) joined by `depends-on`, distinguished by their names.

sources.description: Matched catalog rows for this slice, each referenced by `{ source, lead }`. At most one entry per `source`: a slice matches leads across sources but never fuses two leads from the same source (the kernel enforces this as `plan-reconcile-slice-source-collision`). A lead may appear in more than one slice — that is fan-out, not a double-count. The kernel writes the set to `plan.yaml.slices[].sources[]`.

rationale.description: Agent's reason for the cross-source match in this slice. The agent renders it into `change.md` for Gate 1 review; the kernel ignores it and does not echo it into the journal. Recommended when the match is not obvious from shared slugs or synopsis content.

depends-on.description: Slice names this row depends on. Each name must resolve to a projected `plan.yaml.slices[]` entry.

project.description: Project this slice binds to, chosen by the agent from the request's `projects[]`. Required on every slice when the request offers more than one project; optional when exactly one project exists, in which case the kernel auto-binds it and derives `target` from that project's entry.
